evals.prompt: We're spending $80k/month on Meta and that's it. ROAS dropped from 3.2 to 1.8 over 4 months. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses single-channel concentration risk plus likely creative fatigue or audience saturation. Walks the channel portfolio framework (no channel above 60% at scale). Walks the exhausted-vs-scalable diagnostic. Recommends creative refresh first, then audience expansion test, then begin diversifying into a second channel. References ad-creative for refresh tactics.
assertions: Identifies single-channel concentration as a structural risk
Walks the channel portfolio framework
Walks the exhausted-vs-scalable diagnostic
Recommends creative refresh before pausing or scaling down
Recommends starting a second-channel test
References ad-creative for refresh tactics
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our TikTok campaign is at 1.2 ROAS for two weeks. Should we pause it?
evals.expected_output: Not yet. Walks the when-to-pause discipline: 2 weeks is the refresh trigger, not the pause trigger. Notes TikTok's 3-day creative-fatigue cycle is faster than other channels and may explain the drop. Recommends 3-5 new creatives in the next week before any pause decision. References ad-creative for the refresh approach.
assertions: Does not recommend immediate pause at 2 weeks
Walks the when-to-pause discipline (3-week pause rule)
Names TikTok's 3-day creative-fatigue cycle
Recommends new creative volume before pause decision
References ad-creative for refresh approach
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: Our last-click attribution says YouTube has 0.8 ROAS so my partner wants to kill it. But our multi-touch says 2.4. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Walks the attribution-model-aware decisions framework. Notes YouTube is typically upper-funnel and last-click hides contribution. Recommends not pausing on last-click alone. Recommends reconciling across last-click, multi-touch, and (if available) MMM. Notes that where models disagree, customer-journey research breaks the tie. Likely recommends keeping YouTube at current spend pending deeper attribution reconciliation.
assertions: Walks the attribution-model-aware framework
Notes YouTube is upper-funnel and last-click hides contribution
Recommends not pausing on last-click alone
Recommends reconciling across multiple attribution models
References paid-ltv-optimization for the deeper channel evaluation
Output has Quick Wins, High-Impact Changes, Test Ideas sections

evals.prompt: We're at $30k/month total spend, 95% on Meta, 5% testing Google. Should I diversify more?
evals.expected_output: At $30k/month, single-channel-heavy is mostly fine but the testing budget should grow. Walks the portfolio framework's scale-threshold logic (the no-channel-above-60% rule kicks in above $50k/month). Recommends growing the Google test to 15-20% before adding a third channel. References paid-ltv-optimization for the channel evaluation.
assertions: Notes the portfolio rule kicks in above $50k/month
Walks the channel portfolio framework
Recommends growing the Google test before adding a third channel
References paid-ltv-optimization for channel evaluation
Does not recommend aggressive diversification at sub-scale

evals.prompt: What's the brand vs direct-response budget split for a $150k/month spend?
evals.expected_output: Walks the brand-vs-DR framework. At $150k/month (mid stage), recommends 80% DR, 20% brand. Names brand placement examples (podcasts, YouTube pre-roll, OOH if local). Warns that brand investment is hard to measure short-term and looks worthless under last-click, but committing despite measurement asymmetry is the discipline.
assertions: Walks the brand-vs-DR framework
Names 80/20 split for $150k/month
Provides brand placement examples (podcasts, YouTube pre-roll, OOH)
Warns about measurement asymmetry of brand spend
Recommends committing despite measurement difficulty

evals.prompt: Meta CPMs have doubled in 6 weeks at the same audience and creative. Spend the same, conversions down. Why?
evals.expected_output: Audience saturation, creative fatigue, or both. Walks the creative-fatigue cycles by channel (Meta ~2 weeks). Diagnoses that 6 weeks of same creative is well past Meta's fatigue threshold. Recommends new creative as the highest-leverage fix. References ad-creative for refresh design.
assertions: Diagnoses creative fatigue and audience saturation
Walks the creative-fatigue cycle for Meta (~2 weeks)
Notes 6 weeks is well past fatigue threshold
Recommends new creative as highest-leverage fix
References ad-creative for refresh design

evals.expected_output: Depends. Asks about audience (Pinterest works for visual, aspirational, female-skewed verticals — home, beauty, apparel, food). Asks about current channel saturation. Recommends a 5-10% incubation-budget test with success criteria defined upfront. References ad-creative for Pinterest-native creative design.
assertions: Asks about audience fit before recommending
Names Pinterest-fit verticals (visual, aspirational, female-skewed)
Recommends incubation-budget test (5-10%)
Recommends defining success criteria upfront
References ad-creative for Pinterest-native creative design
